element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins with $1M (half equity, half loan) to establish foundational understanding of resource management
Core infrastructure mechanics taught through initial placement and network design
Players learn route management and operational logistics after establishing basic network
Financial mechanics for capital acquisition scale with player understanding of cash flow
AI opponents introduce market pressure and stock-dealing rivalry after player establishes baseline company
Emergent competitive pressure requiring strategic pricing and fleet management decisions
Advanced economic manipulation allowing cargo generation and market control
Regional hazards adding operational risk management layer
Creative tools for experienced players to design custom gameplay scenarios

typical_tension_triggers: Time pressure to build railroad to win
Rival company stock dealings and rate wars
Loan interest accumulation and cash flow crises
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-cycle tension with strategic decision points punctuating extended operations; economic pressure builds gradually rather than through discrete events

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Industry building and advanced route optimization available as optional economic deepening

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-adjacent; player begins with $1M and lays initial track/stations with no opposition pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Rival companies emerge as active competitors; rate wars and stock dealings introduce economic pressure while infrastructure expands
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex financial management dominates; players balance loan interest, bond repayment, and route profitability against time-based win conditions
notable_spikes: First rival company entering the market and initiating rate wars
Large loan maturity requiring refinancing at potentially worse terms
Transition from growth phase to profitability maintenance (mid-to-late game)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game organized by regional maps (U.S. West/Midwest/Northeast, England, Europe, Africa, South America in Deluxe) each with distinct cargo types and economic conditions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: U.S. West vs. African campaign (with region-specific troops cargo)

notable_level_patterns.description: Scenarios spanning different historical eras (first century, second century, future) with period-appropriate locomotives and economic mechanics
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Second Century expansion featuring modern/near-future scenarios with flooded-ocean Earth variant maps

notable_level_patterns.description: Progression from simple single-route networks (early scenarios) to complex multi-industry systems with dozens of concurrent train routes (advanced scenarios)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Deluxe introduces 'next millennium' budget scenarios; RT2 Platinum offers 50+ user-made variants
